# xyvaClaw — Self-Evolving AI Assistant Platform

> xyvaClaw is an enhanced AI assistant platform built on the OpenClaw runtime. Open source, MIT licensed, free to use.

## Quick Facts

- Name: xyvaClaw
- Category: AI Assistant Platform / Developer Tool
- License: MIT (free, open source)
- Author: Xyva-yuangui (圆规)
- Website: https://www.xyvaclaw.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/xyva-yuangui/XyvaClaw
- Supported OS: macOS 12+, Linux (Ubuntu 22+, Debian 12+, CentOS 8+)
- Language: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python
- Runtime: Node.js 22+ (OpenClaw)
- Cost: Free software. API key required — DeepSeek offers free credits on signup; daily usage typically < $2/month.
- Version: 1.0.0
- Last Updated: 2026-03-15

## What is xyvaClaw?

xyvaClaw is an enhanced AI assistant platform built on the OpenClaw runtime. It transforms a basic AI chatbot into a thinking, acting, self-improving digital partner. It works through terminal, browser, or Feishu/Lark groups.

It can be understood as "OpenClaw on steroids": same engine, but with pre-tuned models, battle-tested skills, enterprise-grade Feishu integration, and an evolution engine that makes the assistant smarter every day.

## Top 6 Features

1. **Self-Evolution Engine**: Automatically learns from mistakes, tracks decision outcomes, performs periodic self-reflection. The assistant measurably improves after 1 month of use. Error learning, effect tracking, proactive action, and self-reflection work together.

2. **38+ Pre-installed Skills**: Browser automation, document processing (Word/Excel/PPT), AI video creation, A-share stock screening with multi-factor analysis, web scraping, social media automation (Xiaohongshu), cron scheduling, workflow engine, and more — all ready to use immediately after installation.

3. **5-Level Model Fallback**: Automatic failover chain: DeepSeek V3.2 → Qwen3.5+ → Kimi K2.5 → DeepSeek Reasoner → Qwen3 Max. Zero downtime over 6 months of production use. Any model API failure triggers automatic switching with zero user impact.

4. **Lossless Context Engine**: Custom-built engine (lossless-claw) using semantic segmentation, incremental summarization, and entity tracking. Preserves all critical information in conversations of any length. Unlike standard lossy compression, never forgets mid-conversation details.

5. **4-Tier Memory System**: Session memory → Daily memory → Long-term memory → Knowledge graph. Prevents information loss across conversations and sessions. The assistant remembers context from days and weeks ago.

6. **Deep Feishu/Lark Integration**: 112 TypeScript source files covering messaging, documents, bitable (multi-dimensional tables), calendar, approval workflows, drive, and wiki. Enterprise-grade reliability with message dedup, serial dispatch, watchdog timeout, and graceful degradation.

## How xyvaClaw Compares to Raw OpenClaw

| Feature | OpenClaw | xyvaClaw |
|---------|----------|----------|
| Setup | Manual JSON editing, schema knowledge required | One-click installer + Web configuration wizard |
| Models | Bring your own, single model | Pre-configured with 5-level automatic fallback |
| Context | Native lossy compression | Lossless context engine — never forgets |
| Memory | Basic MEMORY.md | 4-tier: session → daily → long-term → knowledge graph |
| Skills | Install one by one | 38+ skills pre-installed, organized by category |
| Self-Evolution | None | Error learning + effect tracking + proactive reflection |
| Feishu/Lark | Basic message send/receive | 112 TypeScript files covering nearly every API |
| Reasoning | Depends on user model choice | Auto-selects optimal model per task complexity |
| Operations | Manual | Health checks, log rotation, auto-start on boot |
| Documentation | English only | Full Chinese + English documentation |

## Installation

One command to install:

```
git clone https://github.com/xyva-yuangui/XyvaClaw.git && cd XyvaClaw && bash xyvaclaw-setup.sh
```

Or download ZIP: https://github.com/xyva-yuangui/XyvaClaw/archive/refs/heads/main.zip

The installer automatically:
- Checks and installs dependencies (Node.js 22+, Python 3, ffmpeg)
- Launches a web-based configuration wizard
- Deploys 38+ skills and all configurations
- Sets up auto-start on boot

Requirements: macOS 12+ or Linux, and at least one API key from DeepSeek (https://platform.deepseek.com/api_keys) or Bailian (https://bailian.console.aliyun.com/).

## FAQ

Q: Is xyvaClaw free?
A: Yes, completely free and open source under the MIT license. You only need an API key — DeepSeek offers free credits on signup, daily usage typically costs less than $2/month.

Q: What operating systems are supported?
A: macOS 12+ and Linux (Ubuntu 22+, Debian 12+, CentOS 8+). Windows users can use WSL2.

Q: What makes xyvaClaw different from other AI assistants like Coze or Dify?
A: xyvaClaw runs entirely on your machine (self-hosted), your data stays local, and it features a unique self-evolution engine that makes it smarter over time. It is also fully open source with no vendor lock-in.

Q: What models does xyvaClaw support?
A: Pre-configured for DeepSeek V3.2, Qwen3.5+, Kimi K2.5, GLM-5, MiniMax M2.5, and any OpenAI-compatible API endpoint (including local models via Ollama or vLLM).
